q: The standard equation of a circle with centre (h, k) and radius r is:
exp: Standard form: (x−h)² + (y−k)² = r². For a circle centred at the origin: x² + y² = r². The centre is (h,k) and radius is r (not r²).

q: The general equation of a circle is x² + y² + 2gx + 2fy + c = 0. Its centre is:
exp: General circle: x²+y²+2gx+2fy+c=0. Completing the square: (x+g)²+(y+f)²=g²+f²−c. Centre = (−g, −f), radius = √(g²+f²−c).

q: The radius of the circle x² + y² − 6x + 4y + 9 = 0 is:
The equation of a circle with diameter endpoints (1, 2) and (3, 4) is:
exp: Diameter form: (x−x₁)(x−x₂)+(y−y₁)(y−y₂)=0 gives (x−1)(x−3)+(y−2)(y−4)=0 ⟹ x²−4x+3+y²−6y+8=0 ⟹ x²+y²−4x−6y+11=0. Both A and C are correct.

q: The equation of the tangent to x² + y² = r² at point (x₁, y₁) is:
exp: Tangent to x²+y²=r² at (x₁,y₁): T = xx₁+yy₁−r²=0, i.e., xx₁+yy₁=r². This is the standard T=0 formula. Verify: the point (x₁,y₁) lies on the tangent: x₁²+y₁²=r² ✓.

q: The length of tangent from external point (x₁, y₁) to circle x²+y²+2gx+2fy+c=0 is:
exp: Length of tangent from external point = √(S₁) where S₁ = x₁²+y₁²+2gx₁+2fy₁+c. If S₁ > 0, point is outside; S₁ = 0, on circle; S₁ < 0, inside.

q: The condition for a line y = mx + c to be tangent to the circle x² + y² = a² is:
exp: Distance from centre (0,0) to line mx−y+c=0 equals radius a: |c|/√(1+m²) = a ⟹ c² = a²(1+m²). The tangent lines to x²+y²=a² with slope m are y = mx ± a√(1+m²).

q: The normal to a circle at any point passes through:
opts: A) The nearest point on the circle
C) The centre of the circle
exp: The normal to a circle at any point passes through the centre. This is because the radius to the point of tangency is perpendicular to the tangent, and the normal is along the radius direction.

q: Two circles x²+y²=4 and x²+y²−6x+5=0 are:
exp: Circle 1: centre(0,0), r₁=2. Circle 2: x²+y²−6x+5=0 ⟹ (x−3)²+y²=4, centre(3,0), r₂=2. d = 3. r₁+r₂=4 > d=3 > |r₁−r₂|=0. Wait: d=3, r₁+r₂=4, |r₁−r₂|=0. Since 0 < 3 < 4, they intersect. Correction: they intersect at two points.

q: The chord of contact from point (x₁, y₁) to circle x²+y²=a² is:
exp: Chord of contact from external point (x₁,y₁): T = xx₁+yy₁−a²=0, i.e., xx₁+yy₁=a². This chord joins the two points of tangency from the external point to the circle.

q: The equation x² + y² + 2gx + 2fy + c = 0 represents a real circle if:
exp: Radius r = √(g²+f²−c). For a real circle: r > 0, so g²+f²−c > 0. If g²+f²−c = 0, it's a point circle. If g²+f²−c < 0, imaginary circle.

q: The angle in a semicircle is always:
exp: The angle in a semicircle (angle subtended by diameter at any point on the circle) is always 90°. This is Thales' theorem. It means if AB is a diameter and C is on the circle, then ∠ACB = 90°.

q: The centre and radius of circle x² + y² − 4x + 6y + 4 = 0 are:
The equation of circle passing through the origin with centre (a, b) is:
exp: Circle with centre (a,b): (x−a)²+(y−b)²=r². Passes through (0,0): a²+b²=r². So (x−a)²+(y−b)²=a²+b² ⟹ x²−2ax+a²+y²−2by+b²=a²+b² ⟹ x²+y²−2ax−2by=0.

q: Two circles are said to be orthogonal if:
opts: A) They intersect at right angles (2g₁g₂ + 2f₁f₂ = c₁ + c₂)
C) They have the same radius
D) Their centres are at distance r₁ + r₂
exp: Two circles are orthogonal if they intersect at right angles. Condition: 2g₁g₂ + 2f₁f₂ = c₁ + c₂. Geometrically, tangent to one at intersection point passes through centre of the other.

q: The tangent to x²+y²=25 at (3, 4) is:
exp: Tangent to x²+y²=a² at (x₁,y₁): xx₁+yy₁=a². Here a²=25, (x₁,y₁)=(3,4): 3x+4y=25.

q: The condition for two circles to touch internally is:
opts: D) d = |r₁ − r₂| (d = distance between centres)
exp: Two circles touch internally when d = |r₁−r₂|. They touch externally when d = r₁+r₂. They intersect at two points when |r₁−r₂| < d < r₁+r₂.

q: The normal to x²+y²=r² at point (x₁,y₁) is:
exp: The normal at (x₁,y₁) passes through the centre (origin) for x²+y²=r². Equation: y/y₁ = x/x₁, i.e., xy₁ = yx₁, i.e., xy₁−yx₁=0. This is the line through origin and (x₁,y₁).

q: The common chord of circles x²+y²=16 and x²+y²−6x=0 is:
exp: Subtract: (x²+y²−16) − (x²+y²−6x) = 0 ⟹ 6x−16=0 ⟹ 3x=8. The common chord is found by subtracting the two circle equations.

q: The equation of circle with centre (0,0) touching line 3x+4y=10 is:
exp: Distance from (0,0) to 3x+4y=10 is |0+0−10|/√(9+16)=10/5=2. So r=2 and equation is x²+y²=4.

q: The power of a point P(x₁,y₁) with respect to circle x²+y²=r² is:
exp: Power of point P = x₁²+y₁²−r². If positive: P outside. If zero: P on circle. If negative: P inside. Power also equals (PA)(PB) for any chord AB through P, or (PT)² for tangent PT.

q: If a circle passes through (0,0), (a,0), (0,b), its equation is:
opts: D) x² + y² + ax + by = ab
exp: General circle x²+y²+2gx+2fy+c=0. Through (0,0): c=0. Through (a,0): a²+2ga=0 ⟹ g=−a/2. Through (0,b): b²+2fb=0 ⟹ f=−b/2. Equation: x²+y²−ax−by=0.

q: The pair of tangents from (5, 0) to circle x²+y²=9 are real because:
opts: A) The point lies on the circle
B) The tangents are always real
C) The point lies inside the circle
D) The point (5,0) lies outside the circle (5² > 9)
exp: For real tangents from an external point, the point must lie outside the circle. Check: 5²+0²=25 > 9=r². So (5,0) is outside x²+y²=9, and two real tangents exist.

q: The equation of the circle with centre (1,2) and touching the x-axis is:
exp: If the circle touches the x-axis, the distance from centre to x-axis equals the radius. Distance from (1,2) to y=0 is |2|=2. So r=2 and equation is (x−1)²+(y−2)²=4.

q: The radical axis of two circles is the locus of points having:
opts: A) Zero radius tangent to both circles
B) Equal power with respect to both circles
C) Equal distance from both centres
D) Maximum power from both circles
exp: The radical axis is the locus of points with equal power with respect to both circles. For S₁=0 and S₂=0, radical axis is S₁−S₂=0. It is perpendicular to the line joining the centres.

q: How many common tangents do two circles have when one is completely inside the other?
exp: When one circle is completely inside the other (d < |r₁−r₂|): 0 common tangents. Internally touching (d = |r₁−r₂|): 1 tangent. Intersecting: 2. Externally touching: 3. Completely separate: 4.

q: The diameter of circle x²+y²−8x+2y+8=0 is:
The equation of tangent to x²+y²+2gx+2fy+c=0 at (x₁,y₁) is:
exp: Tangent to general circle at (x₁,y₁): T=xx₁+yy₁+g(x+x₁)+f(y+y₁)+c=0. This is the T=0 formula where T is obtained by replacing x² with xx₁, y² with yy₁, x with (x+x₁)/2, y with (y+y₁)/2.

q: The number of circles passing through three non-collinear points is:
exp: Through any three non-collinear points, there passes exactly one circle (the circumscribed circle). Its centre is the circum-centre of the triangle formed by the three points.

q: The angle subtended by a chord at the centre is ___ the angle subtended at any point on the major arc:
exp: Central angle = 2 × Inscribed angle (angle at circumference). This is the inscribed angle theorem. The central angle is twice the angle subtended at any point on the major arc.
